quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. Gadget's "$2,392.00 from only 16 units" at "$149.50 unit price" and West being "roughly 35% below North."

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region and its data grounding, e.g. "Review Sprocket pricing, as its high unit sales contribute little revenue" and "Investigate why West underperforms."

insight_density.why: The prose surfaces non-obvious observations like Sprocket's 198 units yielding the least revenue due to its $4.75 price and Gadget's high revenue-per-unit despite low volume, both actionable beyond restating totals.

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g., Gadget's "$2,392.00 from only 16 units" via "$149.50 unit price" and West "roughly 35% below North."

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure — revenue per product ($2,392.00 from 16 units at $149.50), unit counts, and the gap 'roughly 35% below North.'

specificity.why: All three recommendations name specific products/regions tied to data ('Prioritize Gadget... highest revenue per unit,' 'Investigate why West underperforms,' 'Review Sprocket pricing').
